mnemonics: Happily Seek Specifics (H-S-S): Find-S is a 'Happy' (ignores negative examples) algorithm that 'Seeks' the 'Most Specific' hypothesis.
keyPoints: The algorithm starts with the most specific hypothesis (e.g., h0 = <∅, ∅, ∅, ...>) and generalizes it only when necessary.
It uses a 'general-to-specific' ordering of hypotheses and performs a 'maximally specific' search through this space.
It ignores negative examples entirely; it only uses positive examples to generalize the current hypothesis.
commonMistakes: Assuming it will find the most general hypothesis (it finds the most specific one consistent with the data).
Forgetting that it cannot backtrack; it is a greedy algorithm that moves downward (toward generalization) without undoing previous steps.
Thinking it can handle noise (it is highly sensitive to noise and inconsistent data because a single negative example can ruin the hypothesis).
